---
title: "Monaco Overtake Index"
subtitle: "Is Monaco uniquely processional, or just one of several qualifying spectacles?"
---

## The narrative

> *"Monaco produces processional races — it is uniquely unsuited to overtaking and should
> be dropped from the calendar."*

The hypothesis predicts Monaco would rank as the lowest-overtaking circuit on the calendar
by a clear margin.

---

## Method

**Overtake index** = total positions gained by classified finishers ÷ starters per race.
This normalises for field size and attrition. DNFs and pit-lane starters (grid position = 0)
are excluded — their position changes do not reflect on-track overtaking.

Data covers **2022–2025** (92 races, 22 circuits).

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '../..')

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import scripts.export_data as exp
from src.analysis.overtake_index import (
    overtake_index_per_race,
    overtake_index_by_circuit,
    monaco_vs_field,
)

results_df = exp.read('results_2022_2025')
race_oi = overtake_index_per_race(results_df)
circuit_oi = overtake_index_by_circuit(race_oi)
ranked = monaco_vs_field(circuit_oi)

print(f"{len(circuit_oi)} circuits · {len(race_oi)} races · 2022–2025")
```

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Mean overtake index by circuit, 2022–2025. Higher = more position changes per race. Monaco and Japan highlighted."

colours = []
for _, row in ranked.iterrows():
    if row['is_target']:
        colours.append('#E8002D')   # Monaco — red
    elif 'Japan' in row['race_name']:
        colours.append('#FF9800')   # Japan — amber
    else:
        colours.append('#636EFA')   # Field — blue

fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
    x=ranked['mean_overtake_index'],
    y=ranked['race_name'],
    orientation='h',
    marker_color=colours,
    customdata=ranked[['races', 'mean_positions_gained']].values,
    hovertemplate=(
        '<b>%{y}</b><br>'
        'Mean overtake index: %{x:.3f}<br>'
        'Races in sample: %{customdata[0]}<br>'
        'Mean positions gained: %{customdata[1]:.1f}<extra></extra>'
    ),
))

field_mean = ranked['mean_overtake_index'].mean()
fig.add_vline(
    x=field_mean,
    line_dash='dash',
    line_color='white',
    opacity=0.5,
    annotation_text=f'Field mean {field_mean:.3f}',
    annotation_position='top right',
)

fig.update_layout(
    title='Mean Overtake Index by Circuit — 2022–2025',
    xaxis_title='Mean overtake index (positions gained / starters)',
    yaxis_title=None,
    template='plotly_dark',
    height=700,
    margin=dict(l=200),
)
fig.show()
```

---

## Findings

| Question | Finding |
|---|---|
| Where does Monaco rank among all circuits? | 2nd lowest — behind Japan |
| How does Monaco compare to the field mean? | Considerably lower |
| Are there circuits similarly processional to Monaco? | Japan is the only comparable one |
| Is the narrative supported? | Partially |

**Verdict: Partially supported.**

Monaco is genuinely one of the least overtaking circuits on the calendar — but it is not
uniquely so. The Japanese Grand Prix ranks below Monaco on mean overtake index and yet
attracts little of the same criticism. Both circuits are **qualifying spectacles**: because
grid position is structurally decisive on street and high-downforce tracks, the races reward
Saturday performance rather than Sunday wheel-to-wheel racing.

The narrative holds in the sense that Monaco produces fewer overtakes than a typical round —
but the "uniquely processional" framing does not survive scrutiny. Japan is a parallel case
that the received wisdom tends to ignore.
